decision_summary: The chip peak temperature (89.27°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. The most effective primary lever is to increase the heat spreader conductivity to enhance lateral heat dissipation away from the chip. I will apply a conservative 1.5x multiplier to the spreader conductivity, moving it from 189.0 to 283.5 W/(m*K), which is well within the allowed bounds and step ratio.

changes.reason: Increasing spreader conductivity improves lateral heat spreading, reducing the thermal gradient and peak temperature at the chip location.

expected_effects: Reduction in chip_max_temperature towards the 85.0°C constraint
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader layer
risk_notes: Diminishing returns may occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity or boundary conditions rather than lateral spreading.
High conductivity materials may be cost-prohibitive in real-world applications, though this is a simulation study.
